purpose: Normalize HTTP request descriptors into canonical method, URL, header, query, body, and client metadata for web applications and gateways.

endpoints.description: Normalize an HTTP request descriptor without sending it over the network.

sidecar_specification.purpose: Canonicalize HTTP request descriptors for repeatable downstream processing.

agent_usage.when_to_use: Use before routing, logging, signing, test comparison, or gateway policy checks that need stable request shape.
agent_usage.when_not_to_use: Do not use to perform actual HTTP requests, enforce authentication, or apply rate limiting.
agent_usage.example_workflow: 1) Capture incoming request metadata. 2) Call /requests/normalize. 3) Pass the normalized descriptor to validators, analyzers, or gateways.

failure_modes.recovery: Provide an absolute URL or configured base URL before normalization

failure_modes.condition: Header value is not a string or string array
failure_modes.recovery: Convert header values to strings before calling the module

forbidden_behaviors.description: Must not write outside declared scope
Must not make external network calls
Must not perform authentication or authorization decisions

agent_notes.implementation_hint: Use the WHATWG URL class to parse url. Uppercase method, lowercase header names, preserve duplicate query values as arrays, and keep body unchanged. Never call fetch.
agent_notes.known_gotchas: Headers may arrive with mixed case and query parameters may repeat. URL parsing differs for relative URLs unless a base is supplied.
agent_notes.preferred_approach: Create normalizeRequest(input) as a pure function and wrap it in a small HTTP route.
agent_notes.avoid_approach: Do not send requests, do not infer user identity, and do not drop unknown headers.
